gazette: Official Gazette dated 23 July 2023 and numbered 32257
title: The Regulation on Determination of Preferential Origin of Goods in Trade under the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between the Republic of Turkey and the United Arab Emirates has been published.
bodyHtml:
The Regulation on Determination of Preferential Origin of Goods in Trade under the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between the Republic of Turkey and the United Arab Emirates has been published.

Click here for the Agreement.

Click here for the Origin Regulation.

The Regulation will come into effect on September 1, 2023.

Since there is no prohibition on refunds, TEV (Compensatory tax) will not be calculated in exports under the Agreement.

In the trade covered by the Agreement, "Bilateral Cumulation of Origin" can be applied. Due to bilateral cumulation, inputs originating from the other Party country that are included in the production of a final product eligible for preferential treatment under the agreement will be considered as originating from the Party country where the final product is obtained, provided that the working or processes carried out in the exporting Party country exceed the Insufficient working or processing requirement of those inputs.

The Working or processing carried out on non-originating materials that confers originating status are determined in Annex II or Annex II (a).

For proof of origin, TR-UAE Certificate of Origin or Origin Declaration will be used. TR-UAE Certificate of Origin will be prepared only in English and in accordance with customs legislation. The validity period of these documents is one year from the date of issuance.

To issue a TR-UAE Certificate of Origin through the electronic system, together with all information and documents indicating that the non-originating inputs used in the production of the goods meet the conditions specified in Annex II or Annex II (a) and the Turkish or UAE originating inputs used in the production of the goods and the working or processes carried out on these inputs, which is necessary to determine the origin of the goods, an application will be made to prove the origin of the goods.

TR-UAE Certificates of Origin that are endorsed by Customs authorities will have the customs declaration number, the name of the customs authority that endorsed the document, the customs seal or stamp, and the endorsement date applied to the "Endorsement" (Approval) field number (11).

TR-UAE Certificates of Origin may also be issued retrospectively or as a second copy.

Approved exporters will be able to issue origin declarations.

Origin declarations are prepared by the exporter in English, typed, stamped, or printed on the commercial invoice, delivery note, or any other commercial document as provided in Annex IV, in accordance with the legislation of the exporting Party country. If handwritten, the declaration must be written legibly in printing ink. In export transactions from Turkey, if the Origin Declaration is subject to subsequent verification, the name of the customs authority where the export was made is added to the end of the text of the Origin Declaration as "Customs Office of Export - ..."

Origin declarations bear the original signature of the approved exporter. However, an approved exporter falling within the scope of Article 36 is not required to sign such a declaration that has been prepared in his own name by his own handwriting, provided that he has submitted a written undertaking to the customs authority of the exporting Party country assuming all responsibility.

An Origin Declaration can be prepared after the export of the products to which the exporter belongs or within one year from the import of the products to which the exporter belongs, provided that it is submitted to the importing Party country.

Authorization of Approved Exporters

The Ministry may grant approved exporter status to real and legal persons who:

Frequently carry out shipments requiring the issuance of TR-UAE Certificates of Origin,

Provide any guarantee to customs authorities to determine the origin status of the goods and fulfill the provisions of this Regulation,

Have not committed serious or repeated offenses related to tax and customs legislation,

Have records that enable customs authorities to monitor their activities,

regardless of the value of the goods, and who have fulfilled the sample Origin Declaration set out in Annex IV.

Third-Country Invoices

The importing Party country customs authority will accept the origin proof document if the sales invoice is issued by a company established in a third country or by an exporter working on behalf of the said company, provided that the goods meet the requirements of the Regulation.

The exporter or authorized legal representative of the goods shall complete box number (4) of the TR-UAE Certificate of Origin in detail as specified in Annex III.

In exceptional cases where invoices issued in third countries are not available at the time of issuance of the origin proof document, the exporter issuing the origin proof document will fill in the invoice number and date specified in Annex III in the relevant box of the TR-UAE Certificate of Origin. The importing Party country customs authority may request the importer to submit invoices and other relevant documents that confirm the transaction made by the exporter to the importer.

Goods Produced in Free Zones

Goods produced in a free zone in Turkey or UAE will be considered as originating and eligible for preferential treatment if they comply with the requirements of this Regulation and the applied labor or process conforms to this Regulation. Upon the request of the exporter, TR-UAE Certificate of Origin will be issued by the relevant customs authority or organizations authorized by the Ministry, and Origin Declaration will be issued by the exporter.
